findings.evidence: `Cofree.lean:56` has `variable {R : Type u} {C : Type v} {M N P : Type w}`, while the existing core API is already polymorphic: `Comodule.lean:104` defines `structure Hom ... (M : Type w) (N : Type x)`.
findings.fix: Split the variables, e.g. `universe u v w x y` with `{M : Type w} {N : Type x} {P : Type y}`, and let the `Comodule.Hom` signatures use their natural source/target universes.
findings.issue: The unbundled cofree API declares `{M N P : Type w}`, so `cofreeMap`, `cofreeLift`, and `cofreeEquiv` only work when all coefficient and source comodules live in the same universe.

findings.evidence: `Cofree.lean:355-356` has `cofree (M : Type v) ... : ComoduleCat.{u, v, v} R C`; the existing bundler is universe-polymorphic, `ComoduleCat.lean:66` has `of (M : Type w) ... : ComoduleCat.{u, v, w} R C`.
findings.fix: Generalize it to arbitrary `M : Type w` and return the appropriate bundled universe for `M ⊗[R] C` (for example by using `of R C (M ⊗[R] C)` with the resulting universe), instead of hard-coding `v`.
findings.issue: The bundled object `ComoduleCat.cofree` only accepts `M : Type v` and returns `ComoduleCat.{u, v, v}`, unnecessarily restricting coefficients to the coalgebra universe.

summary: The cofree construction is mathematically at the right level, but the new API unnecessarily fixes coefficient/source modules to the coalgebra universe or to each other. Generalize the universe parameters before exposing these declarations.
